intuition: The number of ways to reach step n equals the sum of ways to reach step n-1 and step n-2, because you can arrive from either. This is exactly the Fibonacci sequence.
approach: Use bottom-up DP. Start with dp[1]=1, dp[2]=2. For each step i from 3 to n, dp[i] = dp[i-1] + dp[i-2]. You only need two variables instead of an array.
hints: How do you reach step n? Either from step n-1 (one step) or step n-2 (two steps).
This gives a recurrence: ways(n) = ways(n-1) + ways(n-2).
This is the Fibonacci sequence! Use iterative DP to avoid recursion overhead.
commonMistakes: Using naive recursion without memoization — leads to O(2^n) time.
Off-by-one errors: dp[1]=1 and dp[2]=2, not dp[0]=1 and dp[1]=1.
author: chron303
